hooks.command: f=$(jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // empty'); echo "$f" | grep -qE '/design-system/.*\.html$' && printf '%s' "{\"hookSpecificOutput\": {\"hookEventName\": \"PreToolUse\", \"additionalContext\": \"CLAUDE DESIGN GUARDRAIL: this is a LOCAL design-system HTML file. Per AGENTS.md Design Authority, design cards live in the Claude Design project and are created/edited via the DesignSync tool (get_file to ground on the project, then finalize_plan + write_files to build). Local design-system/ files are ONLY the post-approval parity mirror. If you are starting or doing design work, your FIRST move must be a DesignSync call against project 3af2f1ea-428c-49b3-8b02-c066ec0c7452 - never a local Read, never a chat plan of local file paths. If the design is not yet approved IN Claude Design, STOP and build it there first.\"}}" || true

f=$(jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // empty'); echo "$f" | grep -qE '(\.module\.css|/app/(ds|globals)\.css|/app/.*\.tsx|/design-system/.*\.css)$' && printf '%s' '{"hookSpecificOutput": {"hookEventName": "PreToolUse", "additionalContext": "DESIGN CANON PRE-FLIGHT: you are about to edit a design-bearing file. Before writing, load docs/design-canon.md and hold this change to it. Hard checks: (1) TOKENS ONLY - no raw hex, px type sizes, ad-hoc spacing, or hand-rolled shadows; every value is a var(--...) token. (2) NEVER introduce a new color, element, card, panel, or interaction pattern; if one seems needed, STOP and flag it to Randall by name, do not invent it. (3) BANNED: teal-tint paper hover (color-mix teal %, paper), halftone/dot backgrounds, eyebrow/kicker headlines, em dashes in copy. For hover use --reg-box, a hard ink offset, or ds-link. (4) GRID: every margin/padding/gap is a --space-* step. (5) REUSE existing ds-* classes before writing new CSS. (6) This must implement an APPROVED Claude Design card - if none exists for this UI, STOP and ask for design direction. Nothing reaches Randall until it clears the Definition of Ready (canon Section 10)."}}' || true
